item.title: About our sensory room

item.title: About
item.description:
About Sensory Theraphy

Sensory integration is about how our brain receives and processes sensory information so that we can do the things we need to do in our everyday life. (Jean Ayres, 1972) Sensory Integration is part of everyone’s development and learning in the course of ordinary childhood activities. When a person has good sensory integration then they are able to process information automatically and efficiently. Unfortunately for some people, sensory integration does not develop as efficiently as it should and can affect activities of daily living, academic achievement, behaviour or social participation. (https://www.royalfree.nhs.uk/services/services-a-z/occupational-therapy-services-for-children-and-young-people/specialist-services/sensory-integration-therapy/)

item.title: Services & Fees
item.description: If you want to understand your child’s sensory needs, help your child to develop tools to manage sensory integration disorders which affect everyday activities Sensory Therapy Ennis is a perfect place to learn more about your child and yourself as well.
